title: Stelis retusiloba
taxonomy: Stelis retusiloba is a species of orchid. It belongs to the genus Stelis, which is part of the family Orchidaceae. The Orchidaceae family is a large and diverse group of flowering plants. Stelis retusiloba is further classified into the subtribe Pleurothallidinae within the Orchidaceae family. This classification is based on its botanical characteristics and evolutionary relationships.

intro: Stelis retusiloba, also known as Stelis, is a type of orchid native to tropical regions of Central and South America, belonging to the family Orchidaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory problems, due to its antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Stelis retusiloba include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In herbal medicine, Stelis is commonly prepared as a tea or tincture to treat infections, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory symptoms, making it a valuable resource in traditional medicine.
